name: QuantConnect
description_short: Open-source algorithmic trading platform with cloud-based research, backtesting, and live deployment in Python and C# across all major asset classes.
description_long:
QuantConnect is an open-source algorithmic trading and quantitative research platform that enables traders to design, backtest, and deploy trading strategies using Python or C#. The platform provides institutional-quality data, a cloud-based IDE, and integration with multiple brokerages for live trading.

The LEAN Algorithm Framework is QuantConnect's open-source engine, available on GitHub and capable of running locally or in the cloud. The cloud platform provides free tier access with backtesting capabilities, while paid plans unlock more backtesting nodes, live trading, and Alpha Streams — a marketplace where algorithm developers can license their strategies to institutional investors.

QuantConnect has added significant AI capabilities through its MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and Ask Mia AI assistant. The MCP server connects large language models directly to the QuantConnect API, allowing users to create projects, run backtests, deploy live algorithms, and manage strategies through natural language prompts. This effectively lowers the barrier to algorithmic trading — non-programmers can develop and deploy strategies by conversing with an AI assistant rather than writing code from scratch. The AI integration works across the QuantConnect cloud platform, local development environments like Cursor and Windsurf, and any IDE with MCP support.

QuantConnect provides tick-level data for US equities going back to 1998, forex data from 2004, crypto data from multiple exchanges, and futures and options data. The platform supports event-driven and scheduled universe selection, portfolio construction, risk management, and execution models. It integrates with Interactive Brokers, OANDA, Coinbase, Bitfinex, and other brokerages for live trading.
pros: Python and C# support with cloud IDE
Free tier is genuinely useful for learning
cons: Steep learning curve — requires programming skills
Cloud computing costs add up for heavy backtesting
Live trading setup can be complex
No visual strategy builder — though AI assistant reduces the need for manual coding
Data outside US equities can be limited on free tier
